revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:八軒家船着場]] [[File:Hachikenya Osaka03-r.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Hachikenya Boat Dock]] '''Hachikenya Boat Dock''' (八軒家船着場) was a mooring facility located in {{ill|Temmabashi Kyomachi|ja|天満橋京町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmabashi Kyomachi|1=Temmabashi Kyomachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60996531}} and {{ill|Kitahama Higashi|ja|北浜東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama Higashi|1=Kitahama-Higashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60996515}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūōku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20652290}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. This article covers the history of '''Hachikenya-hama''' (八軒家浜), a historical dock on the left bank of the former {{ill|Kyū-Yodo River|en|Kyū-Yodo River|qid=Q5367937|lt=Yodo River (old course)|1=Kyū-Yodo River}} (Ōkawa), as well as the modern '''Hachikenya-hama Boat Dock''' (八軒家浜船着場), established in 2008 as a tourist pier. == History == {{center| File:Hakkenya-Funatsukiba.jpeg|Stone monument marking the site of Hachikenya Boat Dock, recognized as a historical landmark by Osaka City. File:八軒屋夕景 (浪花百景).jpg|Evening View of Hachikenya ({{ill|100 Views of Naniwa|ja|浪花百景}})
19th century, by {{ill|Utagawa Kuninaga|ja|歌川国員}} }} Located between the {{ill|Temmabashi Bridge|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmabashi Bridge|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18215296}} and {{ill|Tenjin Bridge (Osaka)|ja|天神橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Bridge (Osaka)|1=Tenjin Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443263}}, the port known as '''Watanabe no Tsu''' (渡辺津) was established by the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Also known as Ōe, Kokufu-no-Tsu, Kubotsu, or Rōtsu, this area became the political center of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} and served as a crucial transit hub for routes to {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|qid=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|qid=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}, {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|qid=Q535065|lt=Koyasan|1=Mount Kōya}}, and the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} via the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage path. As the Osaka Plain developed, the port shifted westward, transforming into an inland river port. Despite changes due to political shifts, the site remained a key docking area through the early modern period. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, eight boat houses lined the river, giving rise to the name '''Hachikenya-hama''' (literally "Eight House Beach"). It flourished as a hub for river transport along the Yodo River, serving as the terminal for {{ill|Sanju-koku-bune|ja|三十石船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanju-koku-bune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, passenger boats connecting Osaka and {{ill|Fushimi|ja|伏見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見区|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1197771}}. Hachikenya-hama appeared in various literary and artistic works, including the travelogue *{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|qid=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|1=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}* by {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|qid=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku|1=Jippensha Ikku}} and woodblock prints from *{{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498549}}* and *Naniwa Hyakkei*. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, river transport declined. The {{ill|Osaka City Tram|ja|大阪市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Tram|1=Osaka City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11440819}} was introduced in 1911, and the Hachikenya tram stop operated from 1923 to 1944. == Hachikenya-hama Boat Dock == File:Hachikenya Osaka01-r.jpg|Kawa no Eki Hachikenya ("River Station Hachikenya") File:八軒家船着場.JPG|Hachikenya-hama Boat Dock with {{ill|Keihan City Mall|ja|京阪シティモール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan City Mall|qq=}} in the background As part of {{ill|Aqua Metropolis Osaka|ja|水都大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aqua Metropolis Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504519|1=Aqua Metropolis Osaka}}'s revitalization efforts, the modern '''Hachikenya-hama Boat Dock''' was established on March 29, 2008, in Temmabashi Kyomachi. On August 1, 2009, the adjacent '''"River Station Hachikenya"''' opened, featuring a sightseeing boat terminal, information center, and restaurants. Though named after the historical Hachikenya-hama, the current dock is slightly offset from the original location due to land reclamation along the river. The dock accommodates sightseeing boats, {{ill|Yakatabune|en|Yakatabune|qid=Q3440056|lt=yakata-bune|1=Yakatabune}} (traditional Japanese houseboats), and {{ill|Osaka Suijō Bus|en|Osaka Suijō Bus|qid=Q7105608|lt=Osaka Suijō Bus|1=Osaka Suijō Bus}} services.[http://kinki-ships.com/members/route_cat/route/u-t-o/area03-03/area04-09/ Hachikenya-hama Boat Dock], Kinki Passenger Ship Association. A riverside promenade extends from the dock to the {{ill|Tenjin Bridge|ja|天神橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Bridge|1=Tenjin Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443263}}. === Access === Located adjacent to {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|qid=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station|1=Temmabashi Station}}. ; '''Railway''' * Temmabashi Station ''' {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|qid=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line|1=Keihan Main Line}} / {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|qid=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line|1=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪メトロ谷町線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Osaka Metro谷町線|1=Tanimachi Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1142773}} ; '''Bus''' * "Temmabashi" Bus Stop ''' {{ill|Osaka City Bus|ja|大阪シティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Bus|1=Osaka City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11441645}} Routes 10, 21, 31, 46, 62 * {{ill|OSAKA SKY VISTA|ja|OSAKA SKY VISTA|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/OSAKA SKY VISTA|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504521|1=OSAKA SKY VISTA}} Sightseeing Bus (drop-off only){{Cite web|url=http://www.kintetsu-bus.co.jp/skyvista/terminal.html |title=OSAKA SKY VISTA|publisher=Kintetsu Bus Co., Ltd.|access-date=2017-11-20}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagataya Kombu Main Store, *"Hachikenya's Past and Present: Starting Point of the Kumano Kodo"* (八軒家の今昔 熊野街道のはじまり) == See Also == * {{ill|Naniwatsu|ja|難波津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwatsu|1=Naniwazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659550}} * {{ill|Horie (Osaka)|ja|難波の堀江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horie (Osaka)|1=Naniwa Canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659516}} * [https://8ken-ya.osaka/ River Station Hachikenya] {{Coord|34.69071|N|135.515882|E|display=title|type:landmark|format=dms}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|八軒家船着場|version=102368816|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八軒家船着場]] [[zh:八軒家船着場]] {{Commons category|Hachikenya Boat Dock}} {{wikidata link|Q11391758}} [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inland water transportation]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Pre-war history of Osaka]] [[Category:Transport in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Transportation history of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Yodo River]] [[Category:Water transport in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
